SEQUENCE.COMMENT: idPreAllocator - maintain a set of pre-allocated transaction ids in the idcache folder on csutil
This service must be run on the scheduler every 5m-60m. It will maintain a proper set of transaction ids in the following two folders:
idcache/current - contains a few thousand pre-allocated and unused transaction id files - this is where the framework runtime goes to get new transaction ids
idcache/ranges - this contains ranges of pre-allocated transaction ids, and must contain many days worth of ids in case of a db or network outage
The framework id cache is filled from the central framework database at southwind so that transaction ids can be unique
across each environment (DEV, TEST and PROD) regardless of where this instance is running, whether central or distributed, or
whether a given node in the cluster is down. These transaction ids are cached in the csutil folder on this instance, but since the
actual ids come from the central Oracle database, this service must connect (across the wide area when necessary) to the central
database, periodically, in order to keep the cache full.
The cache is broken up into two folders, the 'current' folder and the 'ranges' folder. The 'current' folder is where the framework gets
individual transaction ids, one at a time. This folder will contain just a few thousand at a time and is refreshed from the ranges folder
every time this service runs. The 'ranges' folder will contain many thousands of ids that represent many days worth of pre-fetched
transaction ids. It is refreshed directly from the central framework Oracle database each time this service runs, though far less frequently.
-- set up the cache folder structure if it doesnt already exist --
-- check if the settings file exists, and create one if not, using hard-coded values for each environment --

INVOKE.COMMENT: ...settings from file are current_total=%sConfigCurrentTotal%, range_total=%sConfigRangeTotal%, range_alloc_min=%sConfigRangeAllocMin%, range_alloc_max=%sConfigRangeAllocMax%

Values.value: ...settings from file are current_total=%sConfigCurrentTotal%, range_total=%sConfigRangeTotal%, range_alloc_min=%sConfigRangeAllocMin%, range_alloc_max=%sConfigRangeAllocMax%

BRANCH.COMMENT: create a set of default values

INVOKE.COMMENT: # idPreAllocator-settings.txt - this is the config file for the idPreAllocator service

Values.value: # idPreAllocator-settings.txt - this is the config file for the idPreAllocator service

INVOKE.COMMENT: ...settings from hard-coded values are current_total=%sConfigCurrentTotal%, range_total=%sConfigRangeTotal%, range_alloc_min=%sConfigRangeAllocMin%, range_alloc_max=%sConfigRangeAllocMax%

Values.value: ...settings from hard-coded values are current_total=%sConfigCurrentTotal%, range_total=%sConfigRangeTotal%, range_alloc_min=%sConfigRangeAllocMin%, range_alloc_max=%sConfigRangeAllocMax%

LOOP.COMMENT: count up the number of transaction ids represented by files named like this: D00244388-D00244423.txt

INVOKE.COMMENT: split the name into its 2 ids

MAP.COMMENT: strip off the leading environment character D, T or P

INVOKE.COMMENT: ...range check: need a total of sConfigRangeTotal=%sConfigRangeTotal% IDs, found %numIds% IDs

Values.value: ...range check: need a total of sConfigRangeTotal=%sConfigRangeTotal% IDs, found %numIds% IDs

EXIT.NAME: neededIds < 1
EXIT.COMMENT: if we don't need any more ids, then exit the loop

MAP.COMMENT: SELECT DUMMY FROM DUAL FOR UPDATE WAIT 5 - wait for up to 5 seconds to get a lock on the dual table

Values.value: SELECT DUMMY FROM DUAL FOR UPDATE WAIT 5

MAP.COMMENT: sSql = select '%sEnvcode%' || TRANSACTION_ID_SEQ.nextval as SNEXTVAL from dual connect by level <= %idChunk%

Values.value: select '%sEnvcode%' || TRANSACTION_ID_SEQ.nextval as SNEXTVAL from dual connect by level <= %idChunk%

INVOKE.COMMENT: don't commit anything, we simply locked the dual table

EXIT.COMMENT: stop trying to get stuff from oracle

MAP.COMMENT: get the first and last transaction ids dbOutput[0] dbOutput[sMax]

INVOKE.COMMENT: ......range check: allocated %idChunk% IDs from Oracle, ranging from %sNextvalSTART% thru %sNextvalEND%

Values.value: ......range check: allocated %idChunk% IDs from Oracle, ranging from %sNextvalSTART% thru %sNextvalEND%

INVOKE.COMMENT: ...current check: need sConfigCurrentTotal=%sConfigCurrentTotal% IDs, found %numCurrentFiles% IDs

Values.value: ...current check: need sConfigCurrentTotal=%sConfigCurrentTotal% IDs, found %numCurrentFiles% IDs

SEQUENCE.NAME: numCurrentFiles < sConfigCurrentTotal
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: if the current set has fallen below the minimum, then increase it from the pool folder

INVOKE.COMMENT: split the name into its 2 ids

MAP.COMMENT: strip off the leading environment character D, T or P

INVOKE.COMMENT: ...current allocate: extracted %numAllocated% IDs from the range file %fileList%

Values.value: ...current allocate: extracted %numAllocated% IDs from the range file %fileList%
